questions.text: On healthcare delivery: Should Canada expand private options?
questions.subtext: Public system under strain. Some propose private clinics and insurance.

options.text: Strengthen public-only; ban private parallel system
Mostly public; allow limited private for efficiency
Mixed public-private; competition improves care
Market-based system; government safety net only

questions.text: On mental health funding: Should it match physical healthcare spending?
questions.subtext: Mental health currently 5-7% of budgets. Opioid crisis raging. Underfunded?

options.text: Major increase; fund crisis response and prevention equally
Significant increase; address gaps but realistically
Adequate now; efficiency matters more than dollars
Private treatment better; limit government role

questions.text: On childcare: Should government fund universal $10/day daycare?
questions.subtext: Federal deal with provinces started rollout. Cost: $30B+ annually.

options.text: Universal public childcare; essential infrastructure
Public daycare + subsidies for lower income
Subsidies only; let private market lead
Minimal government role; families decide

questions.text: On housing affordability: What's Canada's best response?
questions.subtext: Prices doubled in decade. Zoning, taxation, public housing debated.

options.text: Strict rent control; heavy speculation taxes
Public/co-op housing; regulate speculation moderately
Deregulate zoning; let developers build
Market solves it; minimal intervention

questions.text: On decarbonization: How fast should Canada transition?
questions.subtext: Net-zero 2050. Oil/gas still significant. Balance climate with jobs.

options.text: Rapid phase-out; massive green investment
Manage transition carefully; protect workers
Oil essential; invest in cleaner tech, not bans
Climate alarmism overblown; growth first

questions.text: On Trudeau's carbon tax: Right policy or economic harm?
questions.subtext: $170/tonne now. Some say effective, others blame for inflation.

options.text: Right approach; market mechanism works
Good idea but rebates inadequate; needs adjustment
Wrong tool; hurts working people unfairly
Economic sabotage; should be abolished

questions.text: On Indigenous self-determination: How far should Canada go?
questions.subtext: UNDRIP implementation. Sovereignty, land rights, resource control debated.

options.text: Constitutional sovereignty; significant land/resource rights
Strong self-governance; resource agreements (within federation)
Equal treatment; targeted socioeconomic support
Apologized and invested; move forward

questions.text: On immigration intake: What level best serves Canada?
questions.subtext: Record 2024 intake (1M+). Housing strain, integration concerns debated.

options.text: Keep high intake; diversity strengthens Canada economically
Moderate intake; prioritize integration
Current too high; citizens priority
Skilled only; end general immigration

questions.text: On Trudeau raising immigration targets (2022-2024): Wise decision?
questions.subtext: Targets rose to 1M+ during housing crisis. Good policy or worsened affordability?

options.text: Right; strengthens economy and demographics long-term
Good intent but timing poor; should have waited
Mistake; worsened housing and wage pressure
Catastrophic policy; responsible for affordability collapse

questions.text: On federal-provincial balance: Should Ottawa have more power?
questions.subtext: Healthcare, education, environment jurisdictions contested.

options.text: Strong federal leadership; Canada needs unity
Shared jurisdiction; feds set minimums
Provinces should lead; Ottawa focuses on defence/trade
Decentralize; let provinces experiment

questions.text: On inflation crisis: What's the government's responsibility?
questions.subtext: CPI peaked at 8.1% (2022). Groceries, rent soaring. Government response debate.

options.text: Supply chain/global issues; government limited role
Some government responsibility; stimulus was too much
Bank of Canada should have raised rates faster
Price controls and subsidies needed

questions.text: On school curriculum: What should be taught?
questions.subtext: Debates: sex ed, gender/sexuality, CRT, Indigenous history.

options.text: Comprehensive progressive curriculum; decolonize and include all
Balance traditional and progressive; evidence-based, age-appropriate
Focus on basics; parents decide values
Protect from ideological indoctrination

questions.text: On university costs and student debt: What's the solution?
questions.subtext: Average debt: $28K. Tuition rising. Debt forgiveness debated.

options.text: Free tuition; universal higher education access
Subsidized tuition; expand support for low-income
Student debt manageable; education is investment
Market competition; minimize government role

questions.text: On gun control: Where's Canada's balance right?
questions.subtext: Stricter than US. Handgun bans proposed. Enforcement debates.

options.text: Much stricter; phase out handguns and semi-automatics
Current regulations about right; focus enforcement
Over-regulated; legal owners responsible; focus criminals
Americans right; self-defence fundamental

questions.text: On criminal justice approach: What works?
questions.subtext: Violent crime rising. Debates: enforcement vs. root causes vs. rehabilitation.

options.text: Address root causes (poverty, addiction); decriminalize
Balance enforcement with rehabilitation and accountability
Get tough; enforce laws strictly
Law and order first; police need resources

questions.text: On rising gang violence (Toronto, Vancouver): Government response adequate?
questions.subtext: Shootings increased 2021-2023. Some blame weak enforcement.

options.text: Serious; needs major law enforcement coordination
Real but manageable; local policing and social investment
Overstated; historically low; address root causes
Police hamstrung by progressive policies; tougher enforcement

questions.text: On corporate taxation: How should Canada tax business?
questions.subtext: Tech, pharma avoid taxes. OECD minimum 15% coming.

options.text: Raise corporate taxes significantly; redistribute wealth
Close loopholes, moderate rates; fair share but competitive
Lower corporate taxes; attracts investment
Slash corporate taxes; they flee to US anyway

questions.text: On labour power and union strength: What's the balance?
questions.subtext: Union density declining. Gig economy growing. Wage stagnation.

options.text: Strengthen unions; collective power essential
Protect workers; unions good but not absolute
Union power limits competitiveness; individual choice
Unions block efficiency; minimize power

questions.text: On 2023 rail and nurse strikes: How should Trudeau have handled?
questions.subtext: Nurses struck for wages; rail threatened disruption. Mediation vs. forced arbitration.

options.text: Let strikes play out; market determines wages
Government mediation appropriate; balanced settlement
Should have backed unions more; demands were modest
Should have forced arbitration; strikes harm public

questions.text: On abortion access: What's Canada's policy?
questions.subtext: Canada has no federal restrictions. Late-term, parental notification debated.

options.text: Fully unrestricted; bodily autonomy paramount
Unrestricted early; reasonable limits on late-term
Restrictions on late-term; parental notification
Significantly restrict; life begins at conception

questions.text: On LGBTQ+ rights and gender identity: Where should Canada stand?
questions.subtext: Gender-affirming care, conversion therapy bans, drag shows, religious exemptions.

options.text: Actively protect/expand LGBTQ+ rights; no religious exemptions
Protect rights; allow limited religious exemptions
Protect rights but preserve religious freedom
Traditional values priority; limit LGBTQ+ visibility

questions.text: On Trudeau's conversion therapy ban (2021): Appropriate action?
questions.subtext: Criminalized therapy. Religious groups objected; LGBTQ+ groups celebrated.

options.text: Necessary protection; it's abuse
Right goal but implementation could balance religious rights better
Overreach; shouldn't regulate family/religious decisions
Attack on religious freedom; shouldn't pass

questions.text: On NATO and military spending: What should Canada's role be?
questions.subtext: Russia invasion, 2% NATO target, China concerns, NORAD modernization.

options.text: Diplomatic focus; minimize military spending
Meet NATO commitments; strengthen allies
Increase military; Russia and China threats serious
Significant rearmament; NATO must deter

questions.text: On Russia sanctions response (2022): Adequate government action?
questions.subtext: Heavy sanctions imposed but energy sector partially spared. Effective?

options.text: Strong response; effective sanctions on energy/finance
Adequate but could have gone further on energy
Overdone; harmed Canadian economy
Performative; real leverage requires US leadership

questions.text: On Israel-Palestine: How should Canada respond?
questions.subtext: Settlements, humanitarian crises, recognition, arms sales debated.

options.text: Criticize settlements; support Palestinian statehood
Support Israel's security but criticize settlements; two-state solution
Strongly support Israel as democracy and ally
Israel must have security; skeptical of Palestinian governance

questions.text: On trade policy: Free trade or protectionism?
questions.subtext: USMCA, China tariffs, supply chain resilience, Buy Canadian debated.

options.text: Fair trade with labour/environmental standards
Open trade with labour standards
Mostly free trade; protectionism risks harm
Aggressive trade wars; protect domestic

questions.text: On Meng Wanzhou arrest (2018): Was it justified?
questions.subtext: Detained Huawei CFO; China detained two Canadian diplomats. Strategic blunder?

options.text: Right; enforce rule of law even under pressure
Legally correct but strategically costly
Mistake; damaged Canada-China relations
Capitulated to US pressure; endangered diplomats

questions.text: On government spending and deficits: How should Canada manage?
questions.subtext: Debt $35B+ interest annually. Services vs. restraint trade-off.

options.text: Invest heavily; debt manageable if used wisely
Spend on priorities; balance budget over time
Cut spending to reduce debt; smaller government
Urgent austerity; path unsustainable

questions.text: On seniors support: What's Canada's obligation?
questions.subtext: CPP/OAS stable but expensive. Long-term care underfunded. Aging population growing.

options.text: Expand CPP and publicly-funded long-term care
Current system adequate; monitor sustainability
Means-test benefits; reduce for wealthy
Privatize; individuals save for retirement

questions.text: On language and Quebec: How should Canada approach this?
questions.subtext: Bill 101 and Bill 96 limit English. Federal bilingualism debates.

options.text: Protect French; Quebec's measures justified
Both official; bilingual Canada, respect Quebec
Bilingualism wasteful; let provinces choose
Language restrictions undemocratic

questions.text: On Trudeau scandal management: How well did he handle ethical issues?
questions.subtext: SNC-Lavalin (2019), blackface (2019), WE Charity (2020). Pattern of problems?

options.text: Handled fairly well; owned mistakes and moved on
Some poor judgment but no real corruption
Pattern of bad judgment; eroded trust
Corrupt and entitled; should have resigned

questions.text: On supply management (dairy, eggs, poultry): Keep or reform?
questions.subtext: Protects farmers but raises food prices. Trade deals pressure reform.

options.text: Keep full supply management; protect farmers
Gradual reform; transition support for farmers
Eliminate; lower prices benefit consumers
Full market competition; no protections

questions.text: On voting systems: Should Canada change how we elect MPs?
questions.subtext: Trudeau promised electoral reform; broke that promise. PR vs. FPTP debate.

options.text: Proportional representation; fair representation of all
Mixed system; balance clarity and fairness
FPTP is fine; simple and stable
Change destabilizes; keep current

questions.text: On CBC and media funding: What's government's role?
questions.subtext: CBC budget declining. Private media struggling. Misinformation concerns.

options.text: Increase CBC funding significantly; public broadcasting essential
Maintain current CBC funding; modest support
CBC funding adequate; let private market lead
Privatize CBC; too much government propaganda

questions.text: On artificial intelligence regulation: How should Canada respond?
questions.subtext: AI rapidly advancing. Concerns: bias, jobs, copyright, misinformation.

options.text: Strong regulation; mandatory bias testing, transparency, oversight
Moderate regulation; industry self-regulation plus minimums
Light regulation; innovation mustn't be stifled
No regulation; market handles risks

questions.text: On Trudeau's resignation announcement (May 2025): Was it the right time?
questions.subtext: Trudeau announced stepping down as Liberal leader but staying PM temporarily.

options.text: Should have left immediately; prolonged transition destabilizing
Reasonable compromise; allows orderly transition
Smart move; defuses crisis and lets Liberals reset
Never should have resigned; caucus overthrew him

questions.text: On Pierre Poilievre's 'Axe the Tax' campaign: Credible policy or populism?
questions.subtext: Poilievre campaigns to eliminate carbon tax and regulations. Serious or reckless?

options.text: Reckless; abandons climate and worker protections
Populist but reflects legitimate voter frustration on cost of living
Serious fiscal alternative; needs detailed costing
Exactly right; government bloat is destroying affordability

questions.text: On Canada's cost of living surge (2024-2025): Who's most to blame?
questions.subtext: Inflation persists; rent, groceries, utilities soaring. Government vs. global.

options.text: Mostly global supply/interest rate issues; government limited control
Both government spending and external factors; joint responsibility
Government spending worsened inflation significantly
Corporate greed and price gouging; government should intervene more

questions.text: On provincial healthcare system failures (2024-2025): Federal intervention or provincial responsibility?
questions.subtext: ERs closing; waits surging; provinces underfunded or mismanaged?

options.text: Federal must increase transfers and set strict accountability
Increase transfers but let provinces lead implementation
Provinces need to improve efficiency with current funding
Provinces should privatize more; government inefficient

questions.text: On Canada-India tensions (2023-2024): Trudeau's handling of allegations?
questions.subtext: Trudeau alleged Indian agents murdered Sikh activist. India denied; crisis.

options.text: Trudeau right to expose; stand firm on sovereignty
Justified concern but handled poorly; damaged relationship
Overblown accusations; worsened ties unnecessarily
Destabilized geopolitics with unproven allegations

questions.text: On Canada's UN votes regarding Palestine (2024-2025): Correct stance?
questions.subtext: Canada abstained on some votes, voted for Palestinian UN seats. Criticized from both sides.

options.text: Should support Palestinian statehood more strongly; genocide concerns
Canada's balanced position is appropriate
Too anti-Israel; compromises alliance with democracy
Virtue signalling; shouldn't take sides in foreign disputes

questions.text: On Canada-China trade tensions (2024-2025): How should Canada respond to tariffs?
questions.subtext: China imposed retaliatory tariffs on canola, pork. Canada imposed own tariffs.

options.text: Maintain stand against Chinese unfair practices
Stand firm but pursue diplomatic resolution
Tariffs hurt Canadian farmers; prioritize trade over politics
Economic warfare with China hurts Canada most

questions.text: On Syria's re-joining Arab League (2023) and Assad normalization: Should Canada engage?
questions.subtext: Assad's Syria officially welcomed back. Some call for normalization; others demand justice.

options.text: Engage diplomatically; constructive engagement better than isolation
Limited engagement; accountability first before normalization
Maintain isolation; Assad committed war crimes
Arab League decision; Canada shouldn't interfere

questions.text: On Canada's AI regulation pace (2024-2025): Moving too fast or too slow?
questions.subtext: EU and US moving on AI regulation; Canada still drafting policy.

options.text: Regulate quickly before harms proliferate; follow EU lead
Moderate pace with industry input; avoid EU overreach
Light regulation; competition drives safety
Hands-off; AI innovation can't be stifled

questions.text: On Trump 2.0 and Canada-US trade (2025): How should Canada prepare?
questions.subtext: Trump re-elected; threatens tariffs, USMCA renegotiation, border wall cost-sharing.

options.text: Strengthen ties with other nations; reduce US dependency
Negotiate hard but maintain good relationship
Accommodate US demands; too dependent to resist
Tariffs are negotiating tactic; call his bluff

questions.text: On Canada's NATO 2% commitment (2025): Can we afford it during affordability crisis?
questions.subtext: NATO pressure to reach 2% GDP on defence. Canada at ~1.3%. Budget conflict.

options.text: Commit to 2%; global security is non-negotiable
Gradual path to 2%; balance defence and social needs
Increase modestly; can't afford full 2%
Stay at current levels; money better spent at home

questions.text: On Trudeau's immigration target pause/reduction (2024): Correct policy shift?
questions.subtext: Reduced targets from 1M to 885K, then further. Recognition of errors?

options.text: Overdue correction; high intake worsened housing and strain
Reasonable adjustment; admits some error in timing
Unnecessary panic; targets were reasonable
Scapegoating immigrants for government failures

questions.text: On Dental Care Plan rollout issues (2024-2025): Implementation failure or growing pains?
questions.subtext: Promised dental coverage delayed; dentist participation low; eligibility questions.

options.text: Growing pains; important program worth patience
Poor rollout but fixable; shows good intent
Government incompetence; implementation failure
Predictable government waste; shouldn't have tried

questions.text: On cyber attacks disrupting healthcare (2024-2025): Government response adequate?
questions.subtext: UnitedHealth ransomware attack disrupted Canadian healthcare. Cybersecurity failures?

options.text: Exposes need for massive healthcare IT investment federally
Provinces need better cybersecurity; federal coordination helps
Private companies fragile; government should manage systems
Overblown crisis; normal business risk

questions.text: On university Gaza encampments (2024): Balance free speech vs. university responsibility?
questions.subtext: Pro-Palestinian student encampments occupied campuses. Police removed some.

options.text: Universities right to clear disruptions; student access compromised
Allow peaceful protest; remove only if disruptive
Police response was heavy-handed; criminalizing protest
Universities too permissive; need stricter management

questions.text: On Stephen Harper's criticism of judicial independence (2024-2025): Legitimate or assault?
questions.subtext: Harper criticized court decisions on sentencing, immigration. Debate on judicial activism.

options.text: Legitimate debate on judicial philosophy; courts overreach
Valid concerns but rhetoric undermines court independence
Courts making policy; judiciary should follow law
Attack on independence; dangerous to rule of law

questions.text: On Alberta sovereignty movement (2024-2025): Legitimate grievance or separatism?
questions.subtext: Danielle Smith explores autonomy on tax, pensions, immigration.

options.text: Alberta overreach; threatens confederation
Legitimate provincial concerns; negotiate federally
Alberta right to explore more autonomy; feds give too little
Should separate; better off independent

questions.text: On B.C.'s housing and cost of living spiral (2024-2025): Provincial/federal failure?
questions.subtext: Record homelessness, affordability crisis. NDP government mismanagement or systemic?

options.text: NDP failed; need Conservative management and market solutions
Mixed: some government failure and structural problems
Systemic housing crisis; needs federal intervention
Market failure; need public housing and price controls

questions.text: On NDP-Liberal pharmacare deal (2024): Good progressive policy or budget burden?
questions.subtext: NDP extracted pharmacare and dental commitments. Coverage expanding but costs rising.

options.text: Excellent social program; investment in healthcare
Good idea but implementation challenging and expensive
Excessive spending; Liberals bought NDP support
Wasteful pork barrel politics; unaffordable

questions.text: On Trudeau's handling of 2022 Convoy and Emergencies Act: Justified or authoritarian?
questions.subtext: Invoked Emergencies Act first time since 1988. Froze accounts. Debate on powers.

options.text: Necessary; convoy blocked economy and posed security threat
Justified but overreaching; weaker measures could have worked
Authoritarian overreach; peaceful protest shouldn't trigger it
Trampled civil liberties; dangerous precedent

questions.text: On response to foreign interference (2023-2024): Adequate transparency?
questions.subtext: Revelations of Chinese election interference. Debate over disclosure timing.

options.text: Government too slow; voters deserved to know earlier
Reasonable timing; balancing security and transparency
Overblown; foreign interference normal in democracies
Security concerns justify delay; transparency less important

questions.text: On Trans Mountain Pipeline expansion completion (2024): Worth the investment?
questions.subtext: $34B project completed. Environmental groups say misguided; Alberta says essential.

options.text: Mistake; money should fund renewables, not fossil fuel expansion
Necessary transitional infrastructure; oil needed during transition
Good investment; economic benefit and energy security
Waste of money; pipeline never needed

questions.text: On Canada-Colombia deportation dispute (2024): Government right to cancel flights?
questions.subtext: Trump administration tried military plane deportations. Canada paused flights in protest.

options.text: Right stance; inhumane treatment and violation of sovereignty
Made point but pragmatically must cooperate eventually
Grandstanding; Canada should cooperate with US
Weakness; Canada shouldn't challenge US authority

questions.text: On continued oil sands expansion (2024-2025): Compatible with net-zero goals?
questions.subtext: Cenovus, Suncor expanding capacity despite net-zero 2050 targets.

options.text: Incompatible with climate targets; should ban expansion
Tension but necessary during transition; manage emissions
Economic necessity; climate targets too rigid
Oil industry essential; no limits needed

questions.text: On Canada's economic vulnerability (2024-2025): How dependent on US and China?
questions.subtext: Recent disruptions show Canada lacks independent supply chains. Strategic concern.

options.text: Need major domestic capacity in critical sectors; invest federally
Strengthen supply chains but keep trading relationships
Some redundancy okay but global trade more efficient
Self-sufficiency is fantasy; embrace global trade

questions.text: On growing private healthcare in Canada (2024-2025): Inevitable or system failure?
questions.subtext: Private clinics expanding; wait times fuel private demand. Public system breakdown?

options.text: Public system failing; need to accept mixed model
Public system underfunded; fix that before accepting private
Two-tier divides country; must strengthen public
Private choice improves system; allow more private
